answer:
The correct answer is **\boxed{B}**.

When substrate concentration is much higher than \( K_m \) (1000×), the reaction rate is proportional to enzyme concentration. Doubling substrate concentration (now 2000× \( K_m \)) maintains the same \( V_{\text{max}}\)-like rate, but reducing enzyme to \( \frac{1}{3} \) cuts the rate by \( \frac{1}{3} \), so time scales inversely—**9 min → 4.5 min**.
